lessons.title: Sine and Cosine Rules for Any Triangle

content.body:
Sine Rule:
a/sinA = b/sinB = c/sinC

Used when: given two angles and one side (AAS)
Or: two sides and non-included angle (SSA — ambiguous case)

Cosine Rule:
a² = b² + c² - 2bc cosA
cos A = (b²+c²-a²)/(2bc)

Used when: given two sides and included angle (SAS)
Or: all three sides (SSS) to find an angle

Area = ½ab sinC

questions.question: Sine rule is a/sinA =
questions.explanation: Sine rule: a/sinA = b/sinB = c/sinC

questions.question: Cosine rule a² =
questions.explanation: Cosine rule: a²=b²+c²-2bc cosA

questions.question: Area of triangle with two sides 6,8 and included angle 30°
When to use sine rule
questions.explanation: Sine rule needs two angles + one side, OR two sides + non-included angle.

questions.question: When to use cosine rule
questions.explanation: Cosine rule used when: two sides and included angle (SAS) or three sides (SSS).

questions.question: Find c when a=10, b=8, C=90°
Cosine of an obtuse angle is
questions.explanation: Cosine is negative for angles between 90° and 180°. This makes cosine rule give larger c² when angle is obtuse.

questions.question: In triangle with a=5, sinA=0.5, b=8. Using sine rule: sinB =
questions.explanation: sinB/b = sinA/a. sinB = 8×0.5/5 = 0.8

questions.question: Ambiguous case (SSA) in sine rule means
questions.explanation: With SSA, there may be two possible triangles (one acute, one obtuse angle) — the ambiguous case.

questions.question: Bearing navigation uses sine/cosine rules to find
options: distances and angles in navigation problems
questions.answer: distances and angles in navigation problems
questions.explanation: Sine and cosine rules solve real-world navigation problems involving triangles: ship distances, aircraft paths.

questions.question: What does simplifying an expression mean?
options: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.answer: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.explanation: Simplification means rewriting an expression in its simplest equivalent form by collecting like terms and applying arithmetic.

questions.question: Which of these is an example of a variable?
questions.explanation: A variable is a letter (like x, y, n) that represents an unknown or changing value.

questions.question: What is 2x + 3x simplified?
questions.explanation: Collect like terms: 2x + 3x = 5x (both terms have the same variable x).

questions.question: Solve for x: x + 7 = 15
questions.explanation: Subtract 7 from both sides: x = 15 - 7 = 8.

questions.question: What is the result of expanding 4(y - 3)?
questions.explanation: Distribute: 4 × y - 4 × 3 = 4y - 12.

questions.question: Which operation reverses addition?
questions.explanation: Subtraction is the inverse (reverse) of addition.

questions.question: What is 3² equal to?
questions.explanation: 3² = 3 × 3 = 9. The exponent 2 means multiply the base by itself.

questions.question: Factorise: 4x + 8
questions.explanation: The HCF of 4x and 8 is 4. So 4x + 8 = 4(x + 2).

questions.question: What is the y-intercept of y = 3x + 5?
questions.explanation: In y = mx + c, c is the y-intercept. Here c = 5.

questions.question: Solve: 3x = 18
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by 3: x = 18 ÷ 3 = 6.

content.body:
Graphs of trig functions:
y = sin x: period 360°, amplitude 1, range [-1,1]
y = cos x: period 360°, amplitude 1, range [-1,1]
y = tan x: period 180°, range all reals, asymptotes at 90°,270°...

Transformations:
y = a sin(bx + c) + d
a = amplitude, 2π/b = period, c = phase shift, d = vertical shift

Solving: sin x = k, find all solutions in given range
Use CAST diagram or symmetry of graph

content.title: Solving Trig Equations
content.body:
Solve sin x = 0.5 for 0° ≤ x ≤ 360°
sin⁻¹(0.5) = 30°
Sin positive in 1st and 2nd quadrants
Solutions: x = 30° and x = 180° - 30° = 150°

Solve cos x = -0.5 for 0° ≤ x ≤ 360°
cos⁻¹(0.5) = 60°
Cos negative in 2nd and 3rd quadrants
x = 180° - 60° = 120° and x = 180° + 60° = 240°

content.title: CAST Diagram
content.body:
All: 1st quadrant (0°-90°): sin,cos,tan all positive
Sine: 2nd quadrant (90°-180°): sin positive
Tangent: 3rd quadrant (180°-270°): tan positive
Cosine: 4th quadrant (270°-360°): cos positive

Mnemonic: All Students Take Calculus

questions.question: Period of y = sin x
questions.explanation: sin x completes one full cycle every 360°.

questions.question: Period of y = tan x
questions.explanation: tan x has period 180° — repeats every half-turn.

questions.question: Amplitude of y = 3 sin 2x
questions.explanation: The coefficient of sin gives the amplitude = 3.

questions.question: Solutions to sin x = 0.5 in [0°,360°]
questions.explanation: sin=0.5 in 1st quadrant: 30°. Symmetric in 2nd: 180°-30°=150°

questions.question: Where is cosine negative?
questions.explanation: Cosine = x-coordinate on unit circle. Negative when x is negative: 2nd and 3rd quadrants.

questions.question: Solve cos x = 0 in [0°,360°]
questions.explanation: cos=0 where unit circle crosses y-axis: at 90° and 270°

questions.question: y = sin(2x): compared to y=sin x, period is
questions.explanation: Period = 360°/b. With b=2: period = 180° (half of 360°)

questions.question: Maximum value of y = 5 cos x - 1
questions.explanation: Max cos x = 1, so max y = 5(1) - 1 = 4

questions.question: Tan x is undefined at
questions.explanation: tan x = sin x/cos x. Undefined where cos x = 0: at 90°, 270°, ...

questions.question: Solve 2 sin x = 1 for 0 ≤ x ≤ 360°
questions.explanation: sin x = 1/2. x = 30° (1st quadrant) and 150° (2nd quadrant).

content.body:
Sine Rule: a/sinA = b/sinB = c/sinC
Use when: two angles + one side, or two sides + non-included angle

Cosine Rule: c² = a² + b² - 2ab cosC
a² = b² + c² - 2bc cosA
Use when: three sides, or two sides + included angle

Area of triangle: A = ½ab sinC

questions.question: Sine rule formula
questions.explanation: Sine rule: each side divided by sine of opposite angle are equal

questions.question: Cosine rule: c² =
questions.explanation: Cosine rule generalises Pythagoras — extra term -2ab cosC adjusts for non-right triangles

questions.question: When is cosine rule used?
options: Three sides known or two sides+included angle
questions.answer: Three sides known or two sides+included angle
questions.explanation: Cosine rule: SSS (find angle) or SAS (find opposite side)

questions.question: Area of triangle with sides 5,8 and included angle 30° =
Triangle sides 5,6,7. Largest angle faces side 7. cosC =
Sine rule used when
questions.explanation: Sine rule best when two angles are known (third can be calculated) and one side given

questions.question: Bearing 060° means
questions.explanation: Bearings measured clockwise from North: 060° is NE direction

questions.question: Triangle: sides 3,4,5. Angle opposite side 5 using cosine rule =
questions.explanation: cosC=(9+16-25)/24=0/24=0, C=90°. Confirms 3-4-5 right triangle

content.body:
Pythagorean identities:
sin²θ + cos²θ = 1
1 + tan²θ = sec²θ
1 + cot²θ = cosec²θ

Double angle formulae:
sin2θ = 2sinθcosθ
cos2θ = cos²θ - sin²θ = 1-2sin²θ = 2cos²θ-1
tan2θ = 2tanθ/(1-tan²θ)

Compound angles:
sin(A±B) = sinA cosB ± cosA sinB
cos(A±B) = cosA cosB ∓ sinA sinB

content.title: Proving Identities
content.body:
Prove: (1-cos²θ)/sinθ = sinθ
LHS = sin²θ/sinθ = sinθ = RHS ✓

Prove: sin2θ/(1+cos2θ) = tanθ
LHS = 2sinθcosθ/(1+2cos²θ-1) = 2sinθcosθ/2cos²θ = sinθ/cosθ = tanθ ✓

content.title: Solving Trig Equations
content.body:
Solve: 2sin²θ - sinθ - 1 = 0, 0°≤θ≤360°
Factor: (2sinθ+1)(sinθ-1) = 0
sinθ = -½ → θ=210°,330°
sinθ = 1 → θ=90°

questions.question: sin²θ + cos²θ =
questions.explanation: Fundamental Pythagorean identity: sin²θ+cos²θ=1 for all θ

questions.question: sin2θ =
questions.explanation: Double angle: sin2θ=2sinθcosθ

questions.question: cos2θ in terms of cosθ only
questions.explanation: cos2θ=2cos²θ-1 (one of three equivalent forms)

questions.question: sin(A+B) =
questions.explanation: Compound angle: sin(A+B)=sinA cosB+cosA sinB

questions.question: Simplify: 1-sin²θ
questions.explanation: From sin²θ+cos²θ=1: 1-sin²θ=cos²θ

questions.question: Solve sinθ=0.5 for 0°≤θ≤360°
questions.explanation: sinθ=0.5: θ=30° and θ=180°-30°=150° (both in range)

questions.question: 1+tan²θ =
questions.explanation: Second Pythagorean identity: 1+tan²θ=sec²θ

content.body: In this lesson, we explore advanced aspects of Trigonometry Advanced. Build on what you have already learned and apply your knowledge to more challenging problems.
Learning Objectives

By the end of this lesson you should be able to: (1) Apply Trigonometry Advanced concepts to complex problems. (2) Use appropriate formulas and methods. (3) Interpret results in context. (4) Connect this topic to related areas of mathematics.
Core Theory

This section of Trigonometry Advanced builds on foundational knowledge. In mathematics, advanced problem-solving requires not just knowing formulas but understanding when and how to apply them. Study each worked example carefully.

content.title: Worked Example 5.1
content.body: Consider a more complex problem on Trigonometry Advanced. Approach: (1) Read and understand the problem. (2) Identify all given information. (3) Determine which concepts apply. (4) Solve systematically, showing all steps. (5) Check your answer is reasonable.

content.title: Worked Example 5.2
content.body: Another type of problem you may encounter: Questions may test your ability to combine multiple concepts within Trigonometry Advanced. Practice identifying the type of problem and the appropriate method before attempting to solve.

Common examination questions on Trigonometry Advanced include: definitions and explanations, numerical calculations, diagram interpretation, and analysis of given data. Make sure you can handle all types.
Summary and Review

You have now covered all lessons in Trigonometry Advanced. Review the key concepts from each lesson, practice past paper questions, and ensure you can apply your knowledge confidently. Good luck in your studies!

questions.question: What does simplifying an expression mean?
options: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.answer: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.explanation: Simplification means rewriting an expression in its simplest equivalent form by collecting like terms and applying arithmetic.

questions.question: Which of these is an example of a variable?
questions.explanation: A variable is a letter (like x, y, n) that represents an unknown or changing value.

questions.question: What is 2x + 3x simplified?
questions.explanation: Collect like terms: 2x + 3x = 5x (both terms have the same variable x).

questions.question: Solve for x: x + 7 = 15
questions.explanation: Subtract 7 from both sides: x = 15 - 7 = 8.

questions.question: What is the result of expanding 4(y - 3)?
questions.explanation: Distribute: 4 × y - 4 × 3 = 4y - 12.

questions.question: Which operation reverses addition?
questions.explanation: Subtraction is the inverse (reverse) of addition.

questions.question: What is 3² equal to?
questions.explanation: 3² = 3 × 3 = 9. The exponent 2 means multiply the base by itself.

questions.question: Factorise: 4x + 8
questions.explanation: The HCF of 4x and 8 is 4. So 4x + 8 = 4(x + 2).

questions.question: What is the y-intercept of y = 3x + 5?
questions.explanation: In y = mx + c, c is the y-intercept. Here c = 5.

questions.question: Solve: 3x = 18
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by 3: x = 18 ÷ 3 = 6.

questions.question: What is the formula for the area of a rectangle?
questions.explanation: Area of a rectangle = length × width.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a like term with 3x²?
questions.explanation: Like terms must have the same variable AND the same power. 3x has power 1, not 2, so it is not a like term with 3x².

questions.question: What is the gradient/slope of a horizontal line?
questions.explanation: A horizontal line has no rise, so gradient = rise/run = 0/run = 0.

questions.question: If a = 4 and b = 3, find 2a + b.
questions.explanation: Substitute: 2(4) + 3 = 8 + 3 = 11.

questions.question: What does the inequality x > 5 mean?
questions.explanation: The > symbol means 'greater than'. x > 5 means x takes values greater than (but not equal to) 5.

questions.question: Solve: 2(x + 3) = 14
questions.explanation: Expand: 2x + 6 = 14. Subtract 6: 2x = 8. Divide: x = 4.

questions.question: The HCF of 12 and 18 is:
questions.explanation: Factors of 12: 1,2,3,4,6,12. Factors of 18: 1,2,3,6,9,18. Highest common factor = 6.

questions.question: What is the LCM of 4 and 6?
questions.explanation: Multiples of 4: 4,8,12,... Multiples of 6: 6,12,... Lowest common multiple = 12.

questions.question: Which number is prime?
questions.explanation: A prime number has exactly two factors: 1 and itself. 7 is prime (factors: 1 and 7 only).
